name: GUT baryogenesis
summary: Heavy gauge or Higgs bosons of a grand unified theory (X, Y bosons of SU(5); leptoquarks of SO(10)) decay out of equilibrium with B-violating, CP-violating amplitudes. The resulting baryon asymmetry survives if the decay temperature is below the mass scale and EW sphalerons do not erase it (typically requires non-zero B-L).

addresses_puzzles.note: Original baryogenesis proposal; largely supplanted by leptogenesis because (a) inflation typically reheats below the GUT scale, (b) pure B-violating asymmetry without B-L is washed out by EW sphalerons. Remains viable in scenarios with high reheating.

touches_benchmarks.note: Same X/Y bosons mediate proton decay; Super-K bounds constrain the baryon-number-violating couplings, indirectly limiting the asymmetry generated.

introduces.note: Building on a GUT framework (which provides the X/Y bosons), GUT baryogenesis adds CP-violating phases in the heavy decay vertices.

history: Sakharov (1967) framing; Yoshimura (1978), Toussaint et al. (1979) implemented in SU(5). Largely supplanted by leptogenesis (1986). Modern relevance: SO(10) GUTs with built-in B-L can revive a GUT-baryogenesis scenario.

references.citation: Yoshimura, 'Unified gauge theories and the baryon number of the universe', PRL 41, 281 (1978)
Toussaint, Treiman, Wilczek, Zee, 'Matter-antimatter accounting, thermodynamics, and black-hole radiation', Phys. Rev. D 19, 1036 (1979)
